title: Data Loss Prevention Policy Violation Trending
dataSources: DLP alert logs, policy evaluation events
spl:
index=dlp sourcetype="dlp:alert"
| timechart span=1h count by policy_name
| where count > 0
description: DLP violations indicate policy gaps or insider risk. Trending by policy, user, and channel supports tuning and incident prioritization.
value: DLP violations indicate policy gaps or insider risk. Trending by policy, user, and channel supports tuning and incident prioritization.
implementation: Ingest DLP alerts from email, endpoint, and cloud channels. Classify by policy and severity. Alert on spike in violations or repeated offenders. Report on top policies and channels.
visualization: Line chart (violations over time), Bar chart (by policy), Table (top users).
knownFalsePositives: DLP hits from authorized exports, scheduled finance or HR reports, or template e-mails with test PII; correlate with the data owner and business process.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: DLP product logs, email/cloud TAs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: DLP alert logs, policy evaluation events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest DLP alerts from email, endpoint, and cloud channels. Classify by policy and severity. Alert on spike in violations or repeated offenders. Report on top policies and channels.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=dlp sourcetype="dlp:alert"
| timechart span=1h count by policy_name
| where count > 0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Data Loss Prevention Policy Violation Trending** — DLP violations indicate policy gaps or insider risk. Trending by policy, user, and channel supports tuning and incident prioritization.

Documented **Data sources**: DLP alert logs, policy evaluation events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): DLP product logs, email/cloud TAs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: dlp; **sourcetype**: dlp:alert. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=dlp, sourcetype="dlp:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by policy_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where count > 0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with certificate authority records, an inventory of endpoints, or `openssl x509 -text` on a sample file so expiry and subject data in Splunk match the source of truth.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (violations over time), Bar chart (by policy), Table (top users).
grandmaExplanation: We trend DLP policy hits so patterns of data misuse or misconfiguration are obvious before they turn into a major breach.
